[[Category:kk]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[sn:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[sn:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[sn:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karanogosho Cave Tomb}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[sn:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[sn:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karanogosho Cave Tomb}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". {{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft|12=draft}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341013.htm Tochigi Department of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/bunka.html Nakagawa town home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[gable roof]]. Within is a [[burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Karasuyama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.tochigi-edu.ed.jp/center/bunkazai/1341013.htm Tochigi Department of Education home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/bunka.html Nakagawa town home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[:en:Gable roof|Gable roof]]. Within is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[Taira no Masakado]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[Karasuyama Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[Karasuyama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[:en:Gable roof|Gable roof]]. Within is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Karasuyama Station|Karasuyama Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Karasuyama Line|Karasuyama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[:en:Gable roof|Gable roof]]. Within is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Karasuyama Station|Karasuyama Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Karasuyama Line|Karasuyama Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[ja:唐御所横穴]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[:en:Gable roof|Gable roof]]. Within is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Karasuyama Station|Karasuyama Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Karasuyama Line|Karasuyama Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[ja:唐御所横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[ja:唐御所横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[ja:唐御所横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Karanogosho Cave Tomb]][[ja:唐御所横穴]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | native_name =唐御所横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Karanogosho yokoana kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Karanogosho Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|37.6|N|140|9|23.9|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Cave Tomb '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi |Nakagawa]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[:en:Gable roof|Gable roof]]. Within is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Karasuyama Station|Karasuyama Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Karasuyama Line|Karasuyama Line]].

==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a [[:en:Gable roof|Gable roof]]. Within is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of [[:en:Taira no Masakado|Taira no Masakado]] in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Karasuyama Station|Karasuyama Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Karasuyama Line|Karasuyama Line]].

{{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi |en|Nakagawa, Tochigi |WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a {{ill|Gable roof|en|Gable roof|WD=Q149617|lt=Gable roof}}. Within is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|en|Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}} in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Karasuyama Station|en|Karasuyama Station|WD=Q6368662|lt=Karasuyama Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Karasuyama Line|en|Karasuyama Line|WD=Q1067927|lt=Karasuyama Line}}.

= {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Karanogosho Yokoanabo '''|唐御所横穴|Karanogosho yokoana}} is a {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tomb located in the town of {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi |en|Nakagawa, Tochigi |qid=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1934.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171595|title=唐御所横穴 |trans-title= Karanogosho yokoana|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The tomb is one of many which were dug into the southern slope of the {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} hills on the eastern bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. The Karanogosho tomb is presumed to have been built in the 7th century. The ceiling of the entrance vestibule, which is about 2.75 meters long, is made to resemble a {{ill|Gable roof|en|Gable roof|qid=Q149617|lt=Gable roof|1=Gable roof}}. Within is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a 1.9 meter ceiling, 2.34 meter width and 4.78 meter length, containing U-shaped stands for three caskets. The tomb is the most elaborate of the many tombs in the area, and can be viewed by a walkway with facilities for rest and explanation placards.
The tomb is associated in legend with the rebellion of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|en|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|qid=Q1207652|lt=Taira no Masakado}} in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} and is variously claimed to have been used as a cave-prison, or as a hiding place for one of Masakado's daughters, who is claimed to have given birth to a son in this location. Fearing for the infant's safety should his identity be revealed, she claimed to have been a princess from Tang China, hence the name of the tomb was called the ''Kara no Gosho'', or "Tang Palace". There is no documentary evidence to support any of these legends.
The site is about 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Karasuyama Station|en|Karasuyama Station|qid=Q6368662|lt=Karasuyama Station|1=Karasuyama Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Karasuyama Line|en|Karasuyama Line|qid=Q1067927|lt=Karasuyama Line|1=Karasuyama Line}}.
